player_behavior_assumptions.notes: Active players click/interact every 30–60s. Idle players return every 4–8h. Economy must be rewarding for both.

notes: Full economy model for AgriEmpire. Every formula includes tunables (safe_range), provenance, and a Gini/inflation impact annotation. This document is the authoritative input for Simulation AI (S4) validation and live-ops adjustments. Change any field via a numbered patch in /patches/.

coins.description: Primary soft currency. Earned by harvesting crops, machine passive income, and selling processed goods. Spent on all in-game purchases.
coins.inflation_risk: HIGH — primary emission currency. Mitigation: land tax, machine maintenance, seed cost sink.

gems.description: Premium soft currency. Earned via prestige (1 gem per prestige point floor). Optional cosmetic purchase. Never convertible to coins.
gems.p2w_firewall: Strictly cosmetic / bounded QoL. Cannot be exchanged for coins, yield, or prestige points.

tech_tokens.description: Secondary research currency. Earned from Industrial-tier machines (T4+) and rare crop discoveries. Spent on Tier 4–5 upgrades and advanced skill nodes.
tech_tokens.gate_function: Tech Tokens gate the industrial-era content (2h target). Players who skip machines can never reach industrial tier, creating a natural progression gate without a paywall.

reputation.description: Soft score representing market standing. Earned by consistent harvesting frequency and fulfilling trade contracts. Affects sell price multiplier (up to +30%). Decays 5% per hour of inactivity (idle soft-cap).
reputation.sink_function: Decay is the primary sink. Creates a retention mechanic: returning players rebuild reputation.

tunable.notes: At 0.2 seeds are nearly free and coin drain is negligible. At 1.0 seeds consume all harvest revenue of the first cycle — punishing early game.

crop_seed_cost.provenance: Stardew Valley: seeds cost ~50–70% of one harvest yield. We target 50% for fast feedback.

crop_harvest_value.formula: value = crop.baseValue * crop.yield * farm_multiplier * machine_multiplier * weather_modifier * reputation_multiplier
crop_harvest_value.notes: Reputation multiplier caps at +30% to prevent infinite sell-price inflation via reputation stacking.

tunable.notes: 1.15 is idle-game standard. >1.25 creates hard walls at level 8+.

upgrade_cost.provenance: AdVenture Capitalist: 1.07–1.15. Realm Grinder: 1.12. AgriEmpire targets mid-range.

tunable.notes: 1.12 per upgrade means 10 upgrades = 3.1x income. At 1.25, 10 upgrades = 9.3x — too powerful.
Targets ~10x cost T1→T5. At 1.30: T5 is 54x T1 base — prohibitive without prestige.

machine_passive_income.notes: CPS target: 0 at t=0; ~5/s at t=15min (2 T1 machines); ~50/s at t=1h; ~500/s at industrial era.

worker_hire_cost.notes: Flat hire cost per worker type. Repeated hire of same type multiplies: hire_cost * (1 + 0.5 * already_hired) — prevents infinite stacking.

tunable.notes: 1.5 means each additional same-type worker costs 50% more. At 3.0, second hire is prohibitive.
1.5 → Lv10 cost = 57× baseCost. At 2.5, Lv5 cost = 97× baseCost — extremely punishing.
0.05/level → Lv10 = +50% domain bonus. At 0.15, Lv5 workers outclass machines.
5M divisor: first prestige ~20h nominal. At 500k players prestige in hour 1 — too fast.

prestige_permanent_bonuses.notes: Hard caps on all bonuses prevent prestige loops from making content trivial. Caps validated by simulation.

processing_chain_multiplier.example: Wheat(5) → Flour(11.5) → Bread(26.5) → Premium Bread(61) → Export Package(140)

tunable.notes: 2.3x/stage means stage 4 = 28x raw value. At 4.0x, stage 4 = 256x — makes raw selling obsolete.
2.5 means region 5 costs ~12× first region. At 4.0, region 4 is prohibitive for early-mid players.

inflation_mitigation.notes: Three-layer inflation control: active sinks drain coins continuously, soft caps slow income growth, and reputation decay discourages pure idle accumulation.

sinks.name: Land Tax
sinks.default_en: Land ownership costs you {{amount}} per hour in taxes.
sinks.notes: Linear drain tied to land overcapacity. A player with 200 acres loses 1% coins/hour. Prevents hoarding land without developing it. Drain caps at 2%/h to prevent punishing players who are genuinely building.

sinks.name: Machine Maintenance
sinks.notes: 0.01% of baseCost per machine per second. A fleet of 10 T2 machines (baseCost ~2000 avg) costs ~2 coins/sec in maintenance. Negligible early, meaningful at industrial scale.

sinks.name: Seed Purchase Sink
sinks.notes: Active players planting continuously spend ~30% of harvest income on seeds. This is the primary active-play sink.

sinks.name: Reputation Decay
sinks.formula: reputation -= reputation * 0.05 * idle_hours; reputation = max(0, reputation)
sinks.notes: 5%/h decay prevents reputation from becoming a permanent unbounded bonus. Caps at 50% total decay per idle session.

sinks.name: Upgrade Cost Escalation
sinks.notes: Each upgrade level costs 15% more. Prevents players from spamming all upgrades in one session. At level 10, cost is 4x base.

sinks.name: Processing Input Fee
sinks.notes: 10% of raw input value charged as a processing fee per stage — machines 'consume' some revenue as running cost. Prevents processing chains from being infinite money multipliers.

soft_caps.mechanism: Each additional CPS point above 10,000 contributes only 10% of face value to actual income.
soft_caps.notes: Prevents T5 machine spam from trivializing prestige target before hour 20.

soft_caps.cap: +30% maximum sell price bonus from reputation
soft_caps.notes: Hard mathematical cap. No amount of reputation stacking yields >1.30x.
All prestige bonuses hard-capped independently. Players who prestige 100+ times do not get infinite power.

soft_caps.cap: max 5 workers of the same type
soft_caps.mechanism: hire_cost * (1 + 0.5 * already_hired); hard block at count ≥ 5
soft_caps.notes: Prevents single-worker-type spam. Forces player to diversify workforce.

inflation_kpi.notes: Ratio <1 means the game is deflationary — players are coin-starved. Ratio >20 means sinks are insufficient and coins become worthless. Current sink set targets ratio of 8–12.

monetization.philosophy: Convenience + Cosmetics only. Zero numeric advantage for paying players.
monetization.gem_earn_rate_free: ~5 gems per prestige run. Typical first prestige: ~20h. So free players earn ~5 gems after 20h of play.
monetization.gem_spend_rate_suggested: Cosmetic purchases: 1–5 gems. QoL: 2–4 gems. Full cosmetic library: ~30 gems total.

allowed_items.effect: Arid desert aesthetic with sand dunes
Extends offline income cap from 4h to 8h
Preview next 3 weather events in event feed

phase_1_hook.trigger_mechanic: Plant wheat (30s growth), harvest for +20 coins
phase_1_hook.design_goal: Immediate reward loop established before first dopamine check-out window.

phase_2_early_farm.target: First machine purchased; first auto-income tick felt
phase_2_early_farm.trigger_mechanic: Accumulate 100c → buy Seed Sorter → see CPS tick
phase_2_early_farm.design_goal: Transition from pure active to mixed play. First idle income dopamine hit.

phase_3_automation.design_goal: Player can leave game and return to coins. Core idle loop engaged.

phase_4_industrial.target: First T3+ machine; first Tech Token earned
phase_4_industrial.trigger_mechanic: Buy Hydroponic Bay (15,000c) → produces Tech Tokens
phase_4_industrial.economy_state: Coins: 50k–500k. CPS: 50–500/s. First processing chain active.
phase_4_industrial.design_goal: Economy moves to a qualitatively different scale. New 'aha' moment.

phase_5_prestige.trigger_mechanic: Player chooses to prestige for permanent bonuses + gems
phase_5_prestige.economy_state: Coins may reach millions. CPS: 500–5000/s. Multiple regions. Reputation capped.
phase_5_prestige.design_goal: Prestige is a deliberate choice, not forced. Second-run is faster and different.
